title: Topology of the mathematical domain: can structure and bridges be recovered from a proof library?
description: Unsupervised recovery of mathematics' domain structure and cross-domain bridges from the Lean4/Mathlib theorem dependency graph (Leiden clustering + bridge detection + historical validation). Reasoning reconstructed from the topology-of-mathematical-domain repo.

glossary.theorem dependency graph: Directed graph over Lean/Mathlib declarations; an edge A->B means A's proof uses B as a premise (extracted from Name anchors in tactic strings). The substrate for all clustering and bridge analysis.
glossary.pure vs mixed cluster: A cluster is pure if one top-level Mathlib module covers >=50% of its nodes (a single domain); otherwise mixed (multi-domain). The largest clusters are mixed.
glossary.bridge zone: A mixed (multi-module) cluster, interpreted as a region where several domains co-develop results.
glossary.operational bridge theorem: A theorem whose proof uses specialized (low in-degree) premises from >=2 distinct pure clusters. The project's core detected object; 34 found after filtering.
glossary.conceptual bridge: Two results that share proof STRUCTURE (e.g. a diagonal argument) without shared premises (e.g. Brouwer<->Lawvere). NOT detected by the premise-based method - the central open problem.
glossary.in-degree depth filter (theta): A premise-specificity cutoff: a bridge must use a cross-cluster premise of in-degree <= theta. theta=2 chosen by the elbow/kneedle of the bridge-count curve, excluding ubiquitous utility lemmas.
glossary.utility connection: A widely-used foundational lemma (high in-degree) appearing across domains; what the in-degree filter is designed to exclude (these caused most of the 280 raw bridges).
glossary.neural complexity (CN): A Tononi-Edelman-INSPIRED heuristic CN = integration (entropy of a cluster's cross-cluster bridge distribution) x differentiation (1 - normalized module entropy). Exploratory, not the exact summation formula. SetTheory scores highest.
glossary.tactic normalization: Abstracting concrete tactics to ~200 strategy tokens (qualified/CamelCase names->@, short vars->?, numerals->#, keywords kept) so proof STRATEGY can be compared across domains independent of surface text.
glossary.Leiden clustering: The unsupervised community-detection algorithm (modularity, seed 42) run on the dependency graph to recover domains; yields 138 clusters that map to Mathlib modules.
glossary.loose vs strict detection: In the historical-bridge validation: loose = flagged by any bridge output; strict = survives the depth filter (theta<=2). 6/8 known bridges are loose-detected, 2/8 strict.
